name: Pico Mirrio Trail
name_original: Pico Mirrio – running circular desde Feria hasta la Sierra Vieja
description: Tackle the difficult 4.9-mile (7.8 km) Pico Mirrio circular jogging route from Feria for panoramic views over Extremadura.
region_intro: Jogging around Feria offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its varied elevation and prominent viewpoints. The region features significant hills, with routes often leading to panoramic vistas such as Pico Mirrio and the Castillo de Feria. Runners can expect challenging ascents and descents, providing a robust workout amidst the natural terrain. The area's running routes are well-suited for those seeking to explore the local geography through active outdoor pursuits. Best jogging routes around Feria The most popular jogging route is Pico Mirrio Trail, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) difficult trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain and views from the peak. Another top favourite among local runners is Puerto de Serranos – el Alcornocal loop from La Lapa, a difficult 15.3 miles (24.5 km) path. This long-distance running trail winds through varied terrain, including the cork oak groves of el Alcornocal. Local runners also love the Castillo de Feria – View of Feria and Pico Mirrio loop from Repsol, a 16.4 miles (26.4 km) trail leading through historical sites and offering expansive views of Feria and Pico Mirrio, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Jogging around Feria is defined by challenging hills, scenic viewpoints, and varied natural landscapes. The network of running routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 196 runners have used komoot to explore Feria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Feria?
A: Feria offers a good selection of running routes, with over 30 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, though most are rated as moderate to difficult due to the region's hilly terrain.

Q: Are there any easy running trails for beginners in Feria?
A: While many routes in Feria feature challenging ascents and descents, there is at least one easy running trail available. The region's landscape is generally characterized by varied elevation, making most routes more suited for those seeking a moderate to difficult workout.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Feria?
A: Jogging around Feria is defined by its diverse landscape, featuring significant hills, varied elevation, and prominent viewpoints. You can expect challenging ascents and descents, providing a robust workout amidst natural terrain, including areas like the cork oak groves of el Alcornocal.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in Feria?
A: Yes, Feria offers several long-distance running trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31470523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Serranos – el Alcornocal loop from La Lapa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15.3-mile (24.5 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31465239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Feria – View of Feria and Pico Mirrio loop from Repsol&lt;/a&gt; spans 16.4 miles (26.4 km).

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Feria?
A: Many of the running paths in Feria are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31470523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Serranos – el Alcornocal loop from La Lapa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34345815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from La Lapa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see while running in Feria?
A: The running routes in Feria often lead to panoramic vistas. You can enjoy views from Pico Mirrio and the Castillo de Feria. Other notable sights in the area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Tierra de Barros&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1581105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Feria Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6458193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Grande, Zafra&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique natural features to see on the trails?
A: Yes, the region boasts natural beauty. Runners might encounter the ancient cork oak groves of el Alcornocal on some routes. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Olive Tree of La Tapada&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument in the vicinity.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Feria?
A: The running trails around Feria are well-regarded by the komoot community, with over 196 runners having explored the varied terrain. Users often highlight the challenging hills, scenic viewpoints, and the opportunity for a robust workout amidst the natural landscape.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31465239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Feria – View of Feria and Pico Mirrio loop from Repsol&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads through historical sites and offers expansive views of Feria and Pico Mirrio. The region also features several castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burguillos del Cerro Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6052765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villalba de los Barros Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored in the wider area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Feria?
A: Feria's climate is typically Mediterranean, with hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running, avoiding the intense summer heat. However, the varied terrain provides options for active outdoor pursuits year-round, with cooler mornings or evenings being ideal during warmer months.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for running with dogs?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations aren't always listed, many natural trails in rural areas like Feria are generally suitable for running with well-behaved dogs, provided they are kept under control and local regulations are followed. It's always advisable to check for any specific restrictions in natural parks or protected areas.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the Sierra de Tierra de Barros?
A: Yes, several routes in the Feria region provide excellent opportunities to enjoy the broader landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Tierra de Barros&lt;/a&gt; is a specific highlight that runners can aim for to experience these panoramic vistas.
tips: Mirrio Peak in the Sierra Vieja is a popular hiking trail in Feria, Extremadura. It's a medium-difficulty hike that offers stunning panoramic views and a circular route from Feria.

faqs.question: Where does the Pico Mirrio circular running route start?
faqs.answer: The circular route typically starts and ends in the picturesque town of Feria. You can find parking within the town, which is known for its whitewashed houses and a fortress crowning a hill.

faqs.question: Is this running route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as difficult and is not recommended for beginners. It features a demanding, continuous ascent from Feria, including a steep, stony section leading to Puerto de la Zorra, and requires significant effort to reach Pico Mirrio.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pico Mirrio circular run?
faqs.answer: The terrain is challenging, characterized by a steep and stony ascent, particularly in the initial sections. You'll be running through a mountainous area, the Sierra Vieja de Feria, which is part of the northernmost foothills of Sierra Morena.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Pico Mirrio circular route?
faqs.answer: The highlight of this route is the complete 360-degree panoramic view from Pico Mirrio, encompassing the picturesque Tierra de Barros and extensive oak groves. You'll also pass through the Sierra Vieja de Feria and enjoy views of the historic town of Feria. Don't miss the View of Feria and Pico Mirrio.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this route?
faqs.answer: Given its challenging nature and location in Extremadura, Spain, the best times to run this route are typically spring and autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential colder conditions in winter, making the demanding ascent more enjoyable.

faqs.question: Is the Pico Mirrio circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed or if there are specific leash rules for this trail. It's advisable to check with local authorities or visitor centers in Feria before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of any required permits or entrance fees for accessing the Pico Mirrio circular running route in the available information. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this running route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the official trail known as [CIMA BD01] Castillo de Feria.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Feria?
faqs.answer: The town of Feria, being a historic and picturesque location, is likely to offer various facilities including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. It's a notable town with whitewashed houses, making it a good base for your run.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this difficult running trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficult classification, steep sections, and continuous ascent, you should bring appropriate trail running shoes with good grip, sufficient water, nutrition, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a fully charged phone. Consider trekking poles for stability on the stony sections.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Pico Mirrio circular run?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this 7.8 km (4.8 miles) circular running route is approximately 1 hour and 5 minutes. However, this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the panoramic views.

waypoints.name: View of Feria and Pico Mirrio
